seo.title: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) implementation check...
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for deploying a robust Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system to production, focusing on retrieval quality, latency optimization, and cost management.

items.title: Define Semantic Chunk Boundaries
items.description: Implement recursive character splitting or markdown-aware chunking to ensure paragraphs and code blocks stay intact rather than using fixed-size character limits.

items.title: Configure Chunk Overlap
items.description: Set a 10-15% overlap between consecutive chunks to maintain semantic context and ensure entities split across boundaries are still retrievable.

items.title: Metadata Enrichment
items.description: Attach source URLs, document IDs, and page numbers to every chunk to enable downstream source attribution and filtering.

items.title: Document Versioning and Deletion
items.description: Implement a mechanism to track document hashes and delete or update stale embeddings in the vector database when source files change.

items.title: Character Sanitization
items.description: Strip HTML tags, excessive whitespace, and non-printable characters from raw text before embedding to reduce noise and token usage.

items.title: Distance Metric Alignment
items.description: Verify that the vector database distance metric (Cosine, Dot Product, or Euclidean) matches the specific metric used to train the embedding model.

items.title: HNSW Parameter Tuning
items.description: Adjust ef_construction and M parameters in HNSW indexes to balance indexing speed against search recall based on your dataset size.

items.title: Batch Embedding Implementation
items.description: Implement batching for embedding API calls to maximize throughput and reduce network overhead during the initial data load.

items.title: Index Backup and Persistence
items.description: Configure automated snapshots or persistence for the vector store to prevent data loss during container restarts or service failures.

items.title: Multi-tenancy Isolation
items.description: Implement metadata filtering or separate collections to ensure users can only retrieve data they have explicit permissions to access.

items.title: Hybrid Search Implementation
items.description: Combine vector search with keyword-based BM25 search to improve retrieval for specific terminology, acronyms, or product IDs.

items.title: Reranking Integration
items.description: Apply a Cross-Encoder reranker to the top-k (e.g., top 20) results to re-order the most relevant context to the top for the LLM.

items.title: Similarity Thresholding
items.description: Establish a minimum similarity score cut-off to prevent the LLM from receiving irrelevant noise when no high-quality matches exist.

items.title: Query Rewriting
items.description: Use an LLM step to transform conversational user queries into standalone search terms, removing pronouns and context-dependent references.

items.title: Latency Budget Enforcement
items.description: Set a hard timeout for retrieval and reranking steps to ensure the total response time stays within acceptable P99 limits.

items.title: Context Window Management
items.description: Calculate and enforce a maximum token limit for retrieved context to avoid exceeding the LLM's context window or incurring excessive costs.

items.title: Groundedness Instructions
items.description: Explicitly instruct the model in the system prompt to only use the provided context and to state 'I do not know' if the answer is missing.

items.title: Citation Requirements
items.description: Prompt the LLM to provide inline citations (e.g., [Source 1]) matching the metadata attached to the retrieved chunks.

items.title: Response Streaming
items.description: Implement Server-Sent Events (SSE) or WebSockets to stream the LLM response to the client, reducing perceived latency for the user.

items.title: Hallucination Check
items.description: Integrate a secondary check or NLI (Natural Language Inference) model to verify that the generated answer is logically supported by the context.

items.title: Retrieval Hit Rate Benchmarking
items.description: Measure how often the correct document appears in the top-k results using a golden dataset of query-document pairs.

items.title: User Feedback Loop
items.description: Capture 'thumbs up/down' feedback in the UI and log it alongside the query, retrieved context, and generated response for future fine-tuning.

items.title: Token Usage Tracking
items.description: Log input and output tokens per request to monitor costs and identify anomalous usage patterns or prompt injection attempts.

items.title: Automated RAG Evaluation
items.description: Deploy tools like RAGAS or TruLens to automatically score faithfulness, relevance, and answer correctness in a staging environment.

items.title: Alerting on LLM Failures
items.description: Set up alerts for high error rates from embedding or LLM providers (429, 500, or 503 status codes).
